summary.hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in the ECL2/TM5 region while avoiding off-target compatibility, thereby improving selectivity margin.
summary.critic_hypothesis: The current design protocol overemphasizes ddG at the expense of structural quality. A balanced approach incorporating pLDDT will likely yield better overall candidates.

summary.hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in the ECL2/TM5 pocket while avoiding off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5.
summary.critic_hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating candidates with strong ddG values but insufficient structural and docking quality. Tightening the ddG threshold and increasing candidate diversity may help achieve a better balance of stability and structural quality.

summary.hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 with SSTR2 ECL2 residues (E192, E193, E195) and introducing hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 to improve SSTR2-specific TM6 interactions will increase selectivity margin by differentiating SSTR2 from SSTR3/SSTR5.
summary.critic_hypothesis: The current design strategy is not generating structurally viable candidates at a sufficient rate. A tighter focus on structural quality and binding stability is needed.

summary.hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in the ECL2/TM5 pocket, while charge-neutral substitutions at position 6 will avoid off-target electrostatic complementarity.
summary.critic_hypothesis: The current design process is generating candidates with poor structural quality and docking scores, suggesting the need for tighter structural constraints and improved stability criteria.
